observations.recommendation:
2.8	We recommended that the Management require the:
General Services Officer to cancel the AREs/Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) issued on the semi-expendable properties and issue ICSs to the end-users to monitor the said items; and
Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary Adjusting Entries to eliminate the semi-expendable properties and their corresponding accumulated depreciation and ensure that future procurement of semi-expendable properties be accounted for in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-006.

3.6	We recommended that the Management:
submit to the office of the Auditor a copy of the MOA on distributed breeding stocks between the Municipality and the farmers;
instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct inventory count every reporting period and prepare and submit inventory reports, including the appraised fair value less cost to sell of the breeding stocks, to the Municipal Accountant; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant, after the receipt of the inventory report from the Municipal Agriculturist, to adjust the balance of the Breeding Stocks account and report gains and losses as required by IPSAS 27.

4.6	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to effect the necessary correcting entries presented in the foregoing discussions in order to correct the misstatements and thereafter to be always guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the IPSASs.

5.5	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the PPE given to different schools, including the accumulated depreciation, from the books of the municipality in compliance with the recognition principle of PPE under IPSAS 45 and thereafter to be always guided by the IPSASs.

6.6	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary restatement in the financial statements and adequately disclose the effects of the prior period adjustments in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 3.

7.6	We recommended that the Management:
create Plantilla position for the Municipal Tourism Officer and hire full-time personnel or designate other personnel, for the time being, to manage the affairs of the Municipal Tourism Office, to enable the Municipal Accountant to concentrate in the Accounting Office’s affairs;
reassess the current composition of office personnel and consider the hiring of additional staff, as needed, subject to the Municipality’s budget restrictions; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit within the reglementary period the Accounts and year-end FS, including the necessary SLs and Schedules, of the Municipality as required by the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

8.8	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare and submit Interim FS to permit the timely detection and correction of misstatements; and
record completed projects on the actual completion date and provide monthly or at the very least, quarterly depreciation on all PPEs of the Municipality.

9.5	We recommended that the Management:
ensure that the terms and conditions stipulated in the POs/Contracts, particularly those stated in the delivery schedule, are strictly enforced;
establish a more robust and efficient monitoring system for tracking the delivery status of procured supplies and materials; and
ensure that liquidated damages are computed and imposed on the defaulting suppliers whenever there are late deliveries of supplies and materials.

10.6	We recommended that the Management
ensure that the unexpended balance of the Mitigation Fund – CO remains in the General Fund books and accounted for as Continuing Appropriations;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries to revert to the General Fund the unexpended balance of the Mitigation Fund – CO that is erroneously transferred to STF; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to adequately disclose in the NFS the details of the LDRRMF as required by COA Circular 2012-002.

11.6	We recommended that the Management require the BAC to:
update and revise the RFQ form used in all procurements undertaken through alternative methods to indicate PhilGEPs Registration Number, ABC, and other terms and conditions of the contract; and
require the suppliers to indicate the brand of goods they are offering and other necessary information that may affect the final evaluation of the offers.

12.5	We recommended that the Management instruct:
the BAC to post in the PhilGEPS website the RFQ, NOA, Contracts/POs and NTP, whenever necessary, for procurement conducted through SVP with ABC exceeding ₱50,000.00 to promote competitiveness, transparency, accountability and efficiency in the procurement process; and
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the printout copies of postings of RFQs, NOA, Contracts and NTP in the PhilGEPS website are included among the supporting documents for payment of procured goods, materials or services.

13.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to:
develop and implement a clear, comprehensive guidelines for the conceptualization and approval of PPAs;
strengthen the coordination between the executive and legislative body to ensure timely enactment of appropriation ordinances; and
ensure the timely implementation of identified PPAs with available funding source to guarantee the maximization of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to obtain the necessary documents as the basis of determining the cost of the property to be reflected in the RPCPPE and in the books of accounts to show the correct balances of the affected asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action. The building remained not recorded. The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that there was no document turned over to the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor exert more effort in locating the Deed of Absolute Sale, which will be the basis of transferring these properties in the name of the municipality in compliance with Sec. 39 (2) of PD 1445 and Sec. 449 of GAAM Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The municipality is still in the process of securing necessary documents in the transfer of the parcels of land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant reconcile the cashbook balances with the general and the subsidiary
ledgers of the cash accounts at month-end pursuant to Section 10, Volume II of
NGAS Manual for LGUs to ensure reliability and accuracy of the balances of the
accounts. Also determine the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary
adjustments where appropriate.

We recommended and management agreed that the Inventory Committee
reconcile the results of the count with the Accounting Office and to effect the
necessary adjustments, where appropriate, in order to reflect the correct
balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that management undertake the disposal procedures
on unserviceable properties as mentioned in Section 79 of P.D. 1445 in order to
reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial
statements.

We recommended and management agreed to reclassify the proper
inventory account of the tangible assets with estimated useful life of more than
one year but small enough to be considered as PPE. An Inventory Custodian
Slip (ICS) shall be prepared upon issuance of small tangible items for
monitoring, control and accountability.

We recommended that the personnel concerned comply with the system
stated therein for the proper recognition of Local Road Networks to its
appropriate account. Also, the Municipal Accountant should disclose in the
Notes to the Financial Statements the Road Network System.

We recommended and management agreed that the GSO-designate and
other officials responsible in the distribution prepare, consolidate and submit the
RIS together with the Deeds of Donation, Memorandum of Agreement,
distribution list/acknowledgement receipt of beneficiaries to the Municipal
Accountant which shall be the basis in the preparation of the Journal Entry
Voucher to record the issuances of inventories.

We recommended that physical inventory be conducted regularly and
prepare a report thereon in order to come up with a correct and reliable amount
in the financial statement.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the financial transaction documents to the audit team to
enable the auditor to make a decision in audit, otherwise notice of suspension will
be issued on the subject transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the
deadline set for submission of accounts to give the auditor reasonable time to
determine the validity, propriety and completeness of the transactions.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible remedies to
collect the long overdue accounts. If despite the exhaustion of the available
remedies for the collection and these remain uncollected, the Municipality
should take all the necessary procedures to facilitate the writing-off of the
dormant accounts as stated under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that
payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with
completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the
propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption;
b. Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill out or indicate
the necessary data or information on the Driver’s Trip Ticket; and
c. Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to prepare, on a monthly basis, the
Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption; and the Municipal Accountant to submit the
aforementioned reports to the Auditor for verification purposes.

We recommended and management agreed that the LGU Inspector
conduct post-repair inspection on motor vehicles and equipment and prepare a
report thereon.

We recommended and management agreed that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of PPE and submit the report for verification and reconciliation of the result of the physical inventory with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Inventory on some PPE was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute and record the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: A total of P21,001,206.35 depreciation was recognized on some PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality create an Appraisal Committee to appraise donated equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: A total of Ten disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports were submitted. Management agreed to implement this the soonest possible time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to record the donated properties because there was no documents/appraisal was made as basis to record the value of the properties

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the vouchers, liquidation reports and documents to support the entries made in the General Journal to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some vouchers and liquidation reports. As of the preparation of this report the Municipal Accountant has submitted disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports amounting to P25,047,190.56.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Most of the cash advances were already liquidated as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Most of the cash advances were already liquidated as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 114 of the NGAS in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Office supplies and Agricultural and Marine supplies were already recorded to their inventory accounts upon purchase.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management retain warranty equivalent to at least 1% of the contract price in the procurement of goods to ensure that the supplier will correct latent or patent defects that may be found within the warranty period.
observations.managementAction: 1% warranty was already retained from the procurement of goods for the year 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require contractors the submission of materials test(s) results before the conduct of inspection by the agency personnel and payment of claims for infrastructure projects so that management will be assured of the quality of materials used.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Most of the infrastructure projects were already supported with Materials Test Results attached to the DVs on final payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract of infrastructure projects and purchase of goods with the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The prescribed form of the ABC was already prepared by the Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement on the 124 cattle (steer) distributed to the livestock farmers. Also submit the list of beneficiaries with their addresses, quantity received with their signatures to support the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert diligent effort to collect the loans granted. observations.managementAction: Complied. For the year 2019, the Municipality was able to collect P34,900, P7,540 for the payment of principal and interest, respectively. P3,438,893.00 was already forwarded to the COA Commission Proper for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate implementation of the projects so that benefits due to the constituents specially the tobacco farmers will be delivered.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. For the year 2019, implementation of projects was 84% as compared to last year's implementation of 21% only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the vouchers mentioned in Annex “C” of this report to enable us to make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports were submitted by the Municipal Accountant. As of the preparation of this report, the Municipal Accountant submitted additional disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports in the amount of P11,952,170.02.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is still on-going. As of December 31, 2019 the accounts has a variance of P18,341.36.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere to the policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The transactions charged to the 20% Development Fund were in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Planning and Development Council ensure that shares from Tobacco Excise Tax be appropriated and disbursed in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Memorandum Circular No. 61A and other issuances to benefit intended farmer beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Disbursements charged to the fund were already in accordance with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Work of Group must perform their responsibilities accordingly and be guided with the DPRI when procuring medicines to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money in sourcing essential medicines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the PMC to strictly monitor all infrastructure projects implemented and prepare and update the required report to contribute to the prompt completion and delivery of services to its constituents. Compute and cause the payment of liquidated damages applicable to the project.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Monitoring of infrastructure projects were conducted on every progress billing of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management weigh the advantages and disadvantages of a “by administration” scheme and straight contracting and thereafter favor what is most advantageous to the government. Further, comply with GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 on future projects to be undertaken by administration.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Subsequent projects implemented by administration were already in accordance with GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as presented in Annex “F”. We also recommended that management ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements as enumerated in Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.4 of COA Circular 2012-01 to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Complied on the transactions for the year 2019. The transactions for the year 2019 were already supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking documents for the transactions listed in Annex “G”. In addition, submit the print-out of posting of the RFQ in the PhilGEPS website. Moreover, RFQ for procurement of goods with ABC above P50,000.00 be posted in the portals required in Annex “H” Section IV (E) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Complied on the transactions for the year 2019. Transactions for the year 2019 were already supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the PhilGEPS Registration Number of the suppliers for the transactions listed in Annex “H”. For the municipality’s subsequent transactions, it is also recommended for the BAC to require suppliers to submit their PhilGEPS Registration Number as a condition for award of the contract as required in Section 54.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Complied on the transactions for the year 2019. Transactions for the year 2019 were already supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality require the identified payors to pay their dues to the municipality. Further, we recommended that management intensify the collection of municipal taxes, permit and regulatory fees, service fees and municipal charges within territorial jurisdiction of the municipality to be able to generate additional funds to finance priority programs.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The Treasury Department was able to collect P4,000.00. Also the Treasury Department started collecting the Driver’s Service Fees on subsequent transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to initiate action to enforce the settlement of the suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedures on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) by directing the Municipal Treasurer to dutifully withhold payment of any money due such person/s.
observations.managementAction: Complied. As of December 31, 2019 the Municipality was able to collect P676,333.79 and P60,600 for the year 2019. The amount was already refunded by the accountable officers through payroll deductions and direct payment to the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the strict compliance to the provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 and require the officials to refund the excess of the RATA received by them.
observations.managementAction: Enforced collection of the excess amount. For the year 2019, the agency had complied with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013. The Municipality was able to collect P18,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency or group for complete documentation of government expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Registration fees on trainings attended were paid to the sponsoring agency or group.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop allowing Job Order employees to attend seminars and trainings to maximize the benefits that the municipality can derive from said seminars and trainings. Instead, send permanently appointed and duly elected government officials and employees for this purpose. In case where a Job Order is deemed warranted attending a seminar, a justification should be attached to each approved travel authority.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The agency stopped the practice of allowing Job Order employees to attend seminars and trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management procure only drugs and medicines included in the PNDF Essential Drug List. In addition, requisition officers for the procurement of drugs and medicines are required to certify as to its conformity with essential drugs list as contained in the Philippines National Drug Formulary, to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Essential and needed medicines that were purchased were necessary and needed in the operation of the Municipal Health Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards committee, immediately look into the unimplemented projects and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects. b. The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects. c. The Municipal Engineering Office improves the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program of Work of unimplemented projects. d. The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The project was already implemented by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the vouchers mentioned in Annex “C” of this memorandum to enable us to make a decision in audit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the BAC to invite in writing the Auditor and the other two (2) observers of private group and NGO in all stages of procurement at least three calendar days before the activity in compliance to Section 13.1 and 13.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: 1. Municipal Treasurer deposits all her collection intact daily or the next banking day with the authorized government depository bank to avoid the accumulation of large balances of the cash on hand account. Carefully analyze the entries made on the accounts to avoid negative balances.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Deposit of collections is done daily except on minimal days.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and persons responsible to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents as listed in the attached annex to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing the Sangguniang Bayan to identify and implement projects, considering that it is not among their functions as cited in R.A. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the strict compliance to the provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 and require the officials to refund the excess of the RATA received by them.

Require the GSO-Designate to issue property tags for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs.

The concerned officials and employees to submit the lacking document.

observations.managementAction: Complied on some of the PPE. Insured some of the PPE with the Property Insurance Fund of GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the following: A. The Municipal Accountant shall check the completeness and validity of the documents before paying; and B. The concerned officials shall submit the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended for the municipality to: • Make a request for the write-off of the P2,968,297.82 to be addressed to the Commission Proper. • Intensify collection of the overdue receivables. Strict collection strategies should be adopted.

For the municipality’s subsequent transactions, it is also recommended for the BAC to require suppliers to submit the documentary requirements listed in Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before any payment is made to avoid audit suspensions. Moreover, RFQ for procurement of goods with ABC above P50,000.00 be posted in the portals required in Annex “H” Section IV (E) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to Warranty Security in order to protect the interest of the municipality and the end-users, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the goods within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.

We recommended that management submit the PhilGEPS Registration Number of the suppliers for the transactions listed in Annex “H”. For the municipality’s subsequent transactions, it is also recommended for the BAC to require suppliers to submit their PhilGEPS Registration Number as a condition for award of the contract as required in Section 54.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the management agreed to formulate strategies and policies that will ensure an efficient and effective collection system.  Likewise, we recommended that management intensify effort towards the realization of a 100% accomplishment on the collection of revenue sources and conduct intensive tax campaign and information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We recommended that the municipality require the identified payors to pay their dues to the municipality. Further, we recommended that management intensify the collection of municipal taxes, permit and regulatory fees, service fees and municipal charges within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality to be able to generate additional funds to finance priority programs.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the required supporting documents and reports as prescribed in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles so that the use of motor vehicles and reasonableness of fuel consumption could be properly evaluated. In addition, it is recommended that controls on the use of fuel for motor vehicles be strictly implemented to avoid wastage of government funds. Monitor consumption in accordance with the issued Manual and memorandum to avoid future disallowances in audit.

We recommended and management agreed to initiate action to enforce the settlement of the suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) by directing the Municipal Treasurer to dutifully withhold payment of any money due such person/s.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the COA Circulars mentioned herein to enable the Auditor to conduct timely review and/or audit and recommend measures on deficiencies noted, if any.

We recommended that no cash advances be granted to an officer or employee unless the previous cash advance granted to him is first settled.
We recommended that management require the officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.
We recommend for the strict compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances.
We recommended for the strict compliance to the provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 and require the officials to refund the excess of the RATA received by them.

We recommended that Management refrain from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency or group for complete documentation of government expenditures.

We recommended that management stop allowing Job Order employees to attend seminars and trainings to maximize the benefits that the municipality can derive from said seminars and trainings. Instead, send permanently appointed and duly elected government officials and employees for this purpose. In case where a Job Order is deemed warranted attending a seminar, a justification should be attached to each approved travel authority.

We recommended that management procure only drugs and medicines included in the PNDF Essential Drug List. In addition, requisition officers for the procurement of drugs and medicines be required to certify as to its conformity with the essential drugs list as contained in the Philippines National Drug Formulary, to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public.

The offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
difference and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the
correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended the following:
1. A physical inventory be conducted immediately to come up with a correct
and reliable figure in the financial statement.
2. For the Municipal Accountant to investigate, analyze and reconcile records
pertaining to the incurrence of abnormal balance and to initiate
immediately adjustments to reflect the correct balances.

We have recommended the following:
1. Impose the necessary and appropriate penalties and administrative
sanctions to bidders who committed any of the acts provided in Rules
XXI, XXII and XXIII of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
2. Strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 or the
Government Procurement Reform Act.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere with the
provisions of RA 9184 to avail of the benefit of public bidding.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly
comply with the provisions of RA9184 in its procurement of goods and
services.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee require
the contractors to submit construction and health program approved by the
DOLE in compliance to the requirement of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended the BAC to invite in writing the Auditor and
the other two (2) observers of private group and NGO in all stages of
procurement at least three calendar days before the activity in compliance to
Section 13.1 and 13.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended that the Disbursing Officer liquidate her cash
advances within the prescribed period to avoid exposure of payroll money to
risks of losses.

We have recommended and the management agreed that collecting
officers remit all collections intact with the designated liquidating officer at the
close of each business day to avoid possible misuse or loss of government funds.

We have recommended the following:
1. Municipal Treasurer deposit all her collections intact daily or the next
banking day with the authorized government depository bank to avoid the
accumulation of large balances of the cash on hand account.
2. Carefully analyze the entries made on the accounts to avoid negative
balances.

We have recommended that Management formulate strategies and
policies that will ensure an efficient and effective collection system. Likewise,
we have recommended that management intensify effort towards the
realization of a 100% accomplishment on the collection of revenue sources
and conduct intensive tax campaign and information drive in order to invite
more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We have recommended and the management agreed to strictly comply
with the requirements of Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to
ensure that all taxes due to the LGUs are collected and require all concerned
contractors to submit their clearance.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and persons
responsible to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents as
listed in the attached annex to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that management prepare and submit the
required supporting documents and reports as prescribed in the Manual on
Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles so that the use of motor
vehicles and reasonableness of fuel consumption could be properly evaluated.

We have recommended and the management agreed that disbursement
of government funds shall always bear the approval of the proper officials to
avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that management discontinue the practice of
allowing the Sangguniang Bayan to identify and implement projects,
considering that it is not among their functions as cited in R.A. 7160.

We have recommended that management refrain and discontinue the
grant of mobile allowances to selected government officials and employees and
adhere with the provisions of COA Circulars 2013-003 and 2012-001.

We have recommended and the management agreed to observe austerity
measures in the utilization of government funds and maximize the use of the
mobile postpaid lines maintained by the municipality to be able to save funds
that could be used to finance other equally important projects of the
municipality.

We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer to stop obligating expenditures without an approved ordinance
or law as provided in Section 4(1) of PD 1445, otherwise, such expenditures
shall be disallowed in audit. Also, for the Municipal Accountant to use the
proper account in recording expenses to present fairly the financial statements.

We have recommended for the strict compliance to the provisions of the
DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 and require the officials to refund the
excess of the RATA received by them.

We have recommended that the management comply with the above
circulars and the proper filling-up of the Purchase Order as one of the
supporting documents in the municipality’s purchases.
